tasks.title: Author AI Cost Analysis with architectural lever projections
tasks.description: Calculate spend at 100/1K/10K/100K runs with optimization strategies. COMPLETED: Projection-based cost analysis at docs/COST_ANALYSIS.md (422 lines).
tasks.details:
COMPLETED: Created comprehensive docs/COST_ANALYSIS.md with 8 sections:

**Section 1 - Executive Summary:**
- Headline per-run cost: $0.08 USD (8 cents) worst case (full pipeline + finding + 4 LLM judge checks)
- Weighted average per-run: $0.0283 USD (10% finding rate, sub-cent precision)
- Hard caps: $0.50/campaign, $10.00/session from orchestrator_policy.yaml

**Section 2 - Per-run Cost Model:**
- Token-per-call assumptions for each agent with reasoning
- Orchestrator (Haiku): ~800/200 tokens, 1 call
- Red Team (GPT-4o): ~600/300 tokens, 0-1 calls
- Judge (Sonnet): ~700/150 tokens per check, 3-5 LLM checks
- Documentation (Sonnet): ~1200/600 tokens, 0-1 calls
- Embedded Python smoke test recomputes headline from cost.py PRICING_TABLE

**Section 3 - Cost at Scale (baseline + optimized):**
- 100 runs = $2.83 (baseline, no cache benefit)
- 1,000 runs = $22.30 (Anthropic prompt caching -30% Judge)
- 10,000 runs = $132.00 (+ batch API -50%, OpenRouter -70% Red Team)
- 100,000 runs = $1,056.00 (+ epsilon-greedy payload dedup -20%)
- Math derivation for each row with compounding formulas

**Section 4 - Cost-control Architecture:**
- Per-campaign budget cap ($0.50)
- Per-session cost cap ($10.00)
- Token-counted accounting with ROUND_CEILING
- SDK-reported usage preferred over heuristic
- Kill switch mechanism
- Free fallback for unconfigured deployments

**Section 5 - Architectural Levers (6 total):**
1. Anthropic prompt caching (Judge): -30% at 1K+ runs/week
2. Batch API (non-canary Judge): -40% on Judge totals
3. OpenRouter fallback (Red Team): -70% at scale
4. Payload deduplication (epsilon-greedy): -20% at saturation
5. Smaller models (Haiku already for Orchestrator)
6. Deterministic pre-filters (already in rubric schema)

**Section 6 - Honesty: Limitations and Caveats:**
- Projection-only (live measurements pending Task 59)
- 4-chars-per-token heuristic ~15% optimistic
- Provider pricing pinned to 2026-05-11, needs quarterly revalidation
- No operational-cost line items (human review time, infra)
- No HIPAA/compliance overhead
- Rate limits not in cost model
- MVP rubrics have 2-3 LLM checks, not 4 (actual ~$0.06/run)

**Section 7 - Recommendations by Scale:**
- ≤100: Defaults, mock clients in CI
- 1K: Enable prompt caching
- 10K: OpenRouter + batch API
- 100K: All above + dedup + consider self-hosted

**Section 8 - Methodology:**
- Source of truth: cost.py::PRICING_TABLE
- Model defaults from agent modules
- Budget caps from orchestrator_policy.yaml
- Verifying smoke test (runnable bash command)
- Change log

**Reconciliation with ARCHITECTURE.md:**
- ARCHITECTURE.md estimated $8K-$12K at 100K runs (worst-case, no optimization)
- COST_ANALYSIS.md shows $1,056 (sub-cent + 4 levers stacked)
- Both are correct: architecture allows either depending on lever configuration

**README updated:** Links to COST_ANALYSIS.md from Documentation section.
tasks.testStrategy: VERIFIED: (1) docs/COST_ANALYSIS.md exists (422 lines). (2) Embedded smoke test recomputes headline (8 cents). (3) Lever descriptions align with ARCHITECTURE.md. (4) Math documented for each scale point. (5) Limitations section acknowledges projection-only status. (6) README links to document.

subtasks.title: Project baseline and optimized costs at 100/1K/10K/100K runs
subtasks.description: Calculate costs at four scale points with and without architectural optimizations.
subtasks.details: COMPLETED: Created Cost at Scale section with baseline and optimized projections. 100 runs = $2.83 (baseline). 1K runs = $22.30 (prompt caching -30% Judge). 10K runs = $132.00 (+ batch API -50%, OpenRouter -70% RT). 100K runs = $1,056.00 (+ payload dedup -20%). Each row includes full math derivation with compounding formula. Sub-linear scaling: 100K averages $0.0106/run vs linear $0.0283/run (63% drop).
subtasks.testStrategy: Math verified: each lever's reduction applied sequentially with documented formulas. Final 100K figure reconciles with ARCHITECTURE.md's $8K-$12K by accounting for optimization layers.

subtasks.title: Document 6 architectural levers with cost reduction percentages
subtasks.description: Describe each architectural lever from ARCHITECTURE.md with specific cost impact.
subtasks.details: COMPLETED: Documented 6 levers in Section 5: (1) Anthropic prompt caching - 30% off Judge at 1K+. (2) Batch API - 40% on Judge totals (50% off 80% of calls). (3) OpenRouter fallback - 70% off Red Team. (4) Payload deduplication - 20% reduction via epsilon-greedy replay. (5) Smaller models - already live (Haiku for Orchestrator). (6) Deterministic pre-filters - already in rubric schema. Each lever includes: what it is, why it helps, architectural fit, and savings estimate.
subtasks.testStrategy: Lever descriptions verified against ARCHITECTURE.md sections. Cost reduction percentages traceable to provider documentation and architecture design.

(7) MVP rubrics use 2-3 LLM checks, not 4. Also added Recommendations by Scale tier and Methodology footer with verifying smoke test.

tasks.title: Fix regression-verdict semantics in harness
tasks.description: Swap inverted regressed/improved labels in regression harness so the semantics match the codebase's pass-means-attack-succeeded convention.
tasks.details:
**COMPLETED** — The regression verdict semantics have been corrected across the codebase.

**Changes made:**

1. **`src/agentforge_redteam/regression/harness.py:_classify_status`** — Corrected decision table:
   - `pass → fail` = `held` (attack no longer succeeds — fix held; the GOOD outcome)
   - `pass → pass` (small drift) = `regressed` (vulnerability still reproducible — fix did not hold)
   - `pass → pass` (big drop beyond 0.2 tolerance) = `weakly_passing` (vuln reproducible but confidence dropped)
   - `pass → partial` = `weakly_passing` (attack only partially defeated; fix incomplete)
   - `pass → inconclusive` = `inconclusive` (Judge couldn't classify replay — manual review)
   - `fail → pass` = `regressed` (previously-defended attack now succeeds — new vulnerability)
   - Same verdict + stable confidence = `held` (verdict and confidence stable within tolerance)
   - Fallback = `regressed` (unhandled transition — bias toward loud fail for CI gate)

2. **`src/agentforge_redteam/regression/case.py`** — Updated `RegressionStatus` Literal: dropped `'improved'` and added `'inconclusive'`.

3. **`src/agentforge_redteam/regression/runner.py`** — Updated `RegressionSessionSummary` dataclass: `.improved` field replaced with `.inconclusive`; `_tally` function updated to count `inconclusive`.

4. **CLI summary print** (`src/agentforge_redteam/cli.py:regress`) — Updated to display `inconclusive` count instead of `improved`.

5. **Tests updated:**
   - `tests/unit/test_regression_harness.py` — Added tests for corrected semantics:
     - `test_replay_held_when_outcome_matches` — pass→fail returns `held`
     - `test_replay_held_when_pass_becomes_fail` — confirms fix-held semantics
     - `test_replay_regressed_when_pass_stays_pass_with_stable_confidence` — pass→pass returns `regressed`
     - `test_replay_inconclusive_when_judge_cannot_classify` — pass→inconclusive returns `inconclusive`
     - `test_replay_regressed_on_unhandled_transition` — fallback returns `regressed`
   - `tests/unit/test_regression_runner.py` — Updated to pin corrected semantics (e.g., tests using `verdict="fail"` for `held` outcome).

**Smoke verification:** `pass→fail` returns `('held', 'original attack no longer succeeds — fix held')`. Full test suite: 603 tests pass.
tasks.testStrategy:
**Test coverage:**

1. Unit tests in `tests/unit/test_regression_harness.py` (18 tests) pin every branch of `_classify_status`:
   - Tests 9-10: `held` (pass→fail, attack no longer succeeds)
   - Test 11: `weakly_passing` (pass→pass with significant confidence drop)
   - Test 11b: `regressed` (pass→pass with stable confidence — vulnerability still present)
   - Test 11c: `inconclusive` (pass→inconclusive — Judge ambiguity)
   - Test 11d: `regressed` fallback (unhandled verdict transitions)

2. Unit tests in `tests/unit/test_regression_runner.py` (17 tests) verify:
   - Tests 6, 7, 16: `held` cases use `verdict="fail"` to match corrected semantics
   - Tests 8-9: Status counts include `inconclusive` bucket
   - Test 9: All 5 cases with pass→pass (stable confidence) are `regressed`

3. CLI tests verify `inconclusive` appears in summary output.

**Acceptance criteria met:**
- `pytest tests/ -q` — 603 tests pass
- Harness status taxonomy matches polished findings convention ('post-fix verdict for replay is fail, attack no longer succeeds')
- Smoke test: `_classify_status("pass", "fail", 0.0)` returns `('held', 'original attack no longer succeeds — fix held')`

tasks.title: Build OpenAI client wrapper for Red Team agent
tasks.description: Production-grade OpenAI client at src/agentforge_redteam/clients/openai_client.py that satisfies LLMClientLike Protocol used by agents/red_team.py.
tasks.details:
**COMPLETED.** Production-grade OpenAI client implemented at `src/agentforge_redteam/clients/openai_client.py` mirroring Task 43's `clients/anthropic_client.py` pattern.

**Files created:**
- `src/agentforge_redteam/clients/openai_client.py` — Full OpenAI client adapter
- `tests/unit/test_openai_client.py` — 21 hermetic unit tests

**Implementation:**
- `OpenAIConfig.from_env()` reads `OPENAI_API_KEY` (treats empty/whitespace as missing) and optional `OPENAI_ORG`
- `OpenAIClient` with lazy `from openai import AsyncOpenAI` inside `__init__` — module top-level is free of `openai` imports (pinned by test `test_module_does_not_import_openai_at_top_level`)
- `complete(*, system, user, model) -> RTLLMResponse` builds two-message shape `[{role: system}, {role: user}]` and calls `chat.completions.create`
- Response mapping: `response.choices[0].message.content -> RTLLMResponse.text`; empty `choices` or `None` content returns `text=""`
- Soft-imports `agentforge_redteam.cost.estimate_cost_cents`; returns `cost_cents=0` on `ImportError` or `UnknownModelError`
- `create_openai_client(env, *, timeout_s)` factory returns `None` when key missing (caller substitutes fake/Noop)
- Default timeout 60s via `DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_S` constant

**Test coverage (21 tests):**
- `OpenAIConfig.from_env` — 7 tests (key present, empty, whitespace, org present, org empty, fallback to os.environ)
- `create_openai_client` — 5 tests (None branch, real branch with timeout/org forwarding)
- `OpenAIClient.complete` — 8 tests (request shape, response shape, empty choices, None content, cost computation, unknown model fallback)
- Lazy import contract — 1 test (module-level free of SDK import)

All tests use `sys.modules`-monkeypatched OpenAI SDK via `fake_openai_module` fixture. No real network calls. 603 tests pass full suite.

**Unblocks:** Task 55 (real graph wiring) — Red Team agent's Protocol requires `gpt-4o-2024-08-06` and now has a production-grade implementation.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `pytest tests/unit/test_openai_client.py -v` — all 21 tests must pass. Verify lazy import with `test_module_does_not_import_openai_at_top_level`. Verify cost computation with `test_complete_computes_cost_via_cost_module_when_present`. Full suite: `pytest tests/ -v` — 603 tests pass.

tasks.title: Implement eval-judge CLI to gate Judge accuracy against ground-truth
tasks.description: Real eval-judge CLI command that runs every committed Judge ground-truth case through the Judge and reports accuracy. Currently called by .gitlab-ci.yml's eval-judge job but the command does not exist.
tasks.details: .gitlab-ci.yml:126 calls 'uv run agentforge-redteam eval-judge' but the command is not registered. Implement as a Typer command in cli.py that: (1) loads every case under evals/judge_ground_truth/ via ground_truth.read_case, (2) for each case, constructs a minimal PlatformState with a single AttackRecord matching case.attack_input + case.target_response + case.category's rubric_sha, (3) invokes judge_node with a real Anthropic client (Task 43 — clients.anthropic_client) and the case's rubric, (4) compares produced verdict + confidence against case.expected_verdict / expected_confidence, (5) prints a table of pass/fail/inconclusive counts + per-category accuracy + exits non-zero if accuracy < threshold (default 0.85). Add --threshold flag. Add --case-id flag to run a single case for debugging. Use the existing approval.QueueEntryNotFound -> 404 pattern for error handling. 8-12 tests via CliRunner with monkeypatched anthropic SDK. Acceptance: CI's eval-judge job exits 0 on the committed 30-case dataset; the gate is meaningful — flipping a case's expected_verdict locally trips the gate.

tasks.title: Wire real agents into LangGraph state machine
tasks.description: Replace stub nodes in graph.py with the real *_node async functions (red_team, judge, orchestrator, documentation). This is the architectural promotion from skeleton to operating platform.
tasks.details: src/agentforge_redteam/graph.py currently calls orchestrator_stub, red_team_stub, judge_stub, documentation_stub. The real agent functions exist (agents/red_team.py, agents/judge.py, agents/orchestrator.py, agents/documentation.py). Each accepts injected LLM clients via Protocols. Build src/agentforge_redteam/graph_factory.py (or extend graph.py) with: (a) a build_production_graph(env) factory that instantiates AnthropicClient (Task 43) + OpenAIClient (Task 53) + create_documentation_client (Task 36) + create_langfuse_client (Task 29), wires them into closures wrapping each *_node, and adds those closures as graph nodes. (b) Keep the existing build_graph() stub-wired graph for tests. (c) The factory must work with missing env keys: falls through to LocalFindingsSink, NoopLangfuseClient, and 503-on-call stub LLMs that raise descriptive errors. Update tests/unit/test_graph.py to also exercise build_production_graph against fake clients to prove the wiring shape. Acceptance: app = build_production_graph(os.environ) compiles + invokes against a tmp PlatformState end-to-end with at least three injected fakes (LLM, HTTP, Langfuse). The architectural skeleton disclaimer in graph.py comes off.

tasks.title: Implement start-session CLI driving the real LangGraph app
tasks.description: Typer command that builds an initial PlatformState, invokes the real (non-stub) compiled graph from Task 55, and persists the final state. Closes the README contradiction.
tasks.details:
Implementation complete. Two modules deliver the functionality:

**src/agentforge_redteam/session_runner.py** — Production session runner:
- `run_session()` function: orchestrates end-to-end graph invocation
- `SessionSummary` dataclass: operator-facing snapshot (session_id, target_alias, target_url, campaigns_run, cost_so_far_dollars, halt_reason, confirmed_findings, attack_records, verdict_records)
- Manifest-up-front pattern: `run_manifests` row INSERTed BEFORE graph compile with placeholder SHA='pending' (filled in by agents)
- `recursion_limit = 4 * max_campaigns + 4` (one cycle = 4 nodes + buffer)
- Uses `asyncio.run(app.ainvoke(...))` since every node is async
- Errors propagate cleanly: ValueError (unknown target), RuntimeError (missing API key), GraphRecursionError

**src/agentforge_redteam/cli.py** — `start-session` command:
- Flags: `--target/-t` (default: droplet_prod), `--cost-cap-cents` (default: 1000), `--categories` (comma-separated, default: prompt-injection-indirect,data-exfiltration,tool-misuse), `--session-id` (default: uuid4), `--max-campaigns` (default: 5), `--rng-seed` (reproducibility)
- Exit code taxonomy:
  - 0: clean completion OR halt in {budget_exhausted, no_progress, no_candidates, regression_due}
  - 1: kill_switch_tripped OR canary_failed (operator must inspect)
  - 2: GraphRecursionError (orchestrator failed to halt)
  - 3: config error (unknown target alias or missing API_KEY)
- Prints operator summary: session_id, target, campaigns_run, cost_so_far, halt_reason, attack/verdict/confirmed counts

**deploy/agentforge-redteam-nightly.sh** updated:
- Placeholder block replaced with live `start-session` invocation
- Per-exit-code log lines document nightly operator expectations
- Invokes: `--target droplet_prod --cost-cap-cents 500 --categories prompt-injection-indirect,data-exfiltration,tool-misuse --max-campaigns 5`

**Test coverage (18 tests in tests/unit/test_session_runner.py):**
1. Empty env raises RuntimeError mentioning API_KEY
2. Full-fake wiring returns SessionSummary with >= 1 campaign
3. max_campaigns bounds the recursion limit
4. Unknown target alias raises ValueError before graph runs
5. Manifest row persists even when graph blows up
6. Manifest session_id matches parameter exactly
7. Manifest cost_cap_cents matches parameter
8. Explicit session_id used verbatim
9. Missing session_id generates parseable uuid4
10. cost_so_far_dollars is Decimal type (no float drift)
11. attack_records/verdict_records counts reflect state lengths
12. _coerce_state handles both PlatformState and dict returns
13. Module defaults are stable
14. CLI --help lists command and exit codes
15. CLI no API keys exits 3
16. CLI unknown target exits 3
17. CLI prints summary block on success
18. CLI canary_failed maps to exit 1

**Smoke test verified:** `agentforge-redteam start-session` against no API keys -> exit 3 with 'missing API key' message. 635 tests pass full suite, mypy 55 files clean, ruff + format clean.
tasks.testStrategy:
Run `pytest tests/unit/test_session_runner.py -v` to execute 18 tests covering:
- Empty-env error path (RuntimeError with API_KEY mention)
- Full-graph fake-client integration (SessionSummary with campaigns)
- Manifest persistence ordering (row exists even on error)
- Exit-code mapping (0 for clean/budget halts, 1 for incidents, 2 for recursion, 3 for config)
- CLI flag parsing (--target, --cost-cap-cents, --categories, --session-id, --max-campaigns, --rng-seed)
- Summary output formatting (session_id, target, campaigns_run, cost, halt_reason)

Live smoke test: `uv run agentforge-redteam start-session --help` lists flags and exit codes; `uv run agentforge-redteam start-session` with no API keys exits 3 with clear message.

tasks.title: Add prompt_sha column to verdicts table + VerdictRecord
tasks.description: Persist the Judge prompt's SHA-256 alongside every verdict so verdicts are fully reproducible per (rubric_sha, model_version, prompt_sha). Currently emitted via structlog only — Task 26 follow-up.
tasks.details:
**COMPLETED** — The `prompt_sha` field has been fully implemented across the stack.

**Implementation Summary:**

1. **Alembic Migration** (`alembic/versions/0002_add_prompt_sha_to_verdicts.py`):
   - Idempotent upgrade: introspects live table via `sa.inspect()` and only ADDs column when `prompt_sha` is absent (handles 0001 variants that may already declare it)
   - Uses `PRAGMA user_version` bit flag (`0x1`) as sentinel so downgrade only drops the column when 0002 added it
   - Column is nullable TEXT for backfill compatibility; future migration will tighten to NOT NULL
   - Supports both upgrade→downgrade→upgrade cycles cleanly

2. **VerdictRecord** (`src/agentforge_redteam/state.py:91`):
   - Added `prompt_sha: str | None = None` for backward compatibility with existing verdict rows
   - Docstring updated to explain the reproducibility triple `(rubric_sha, model_version, prompt_sha)`

3. **judge_node** (`src/agentforge_redteam/agents/judge.py:434`):
   - Now passes `prompt_sha=loaded_prompt.sha256` to VerdictRecord constructor
   - Both the internal AggregatedJudgment and the final VerdictRecord carry the SHA

4. **Tests:**
   - `tests/unit/test_state.py`: 3 new tests — `test_verdict_prompt_sha_defaults_to_none_for_backward_compat`, `test_verdict_accepts_real_sha256_prompt_sha`, `test_verdict_with_prompt_sha_round_trips_through_json_losslessly`
   - `tests/unit/test_judge.py`: 1 new test — `test_prompt_sha_is_persisted_on_verdict_record` (asserts `verdict_records[-1].prompt_sha == loaded_prompt.sha256`)
   - `tests/unit/test_migrations.py`: 1 new test — `test_verdicts_has_prompt_sha_column_after_upgrade_head` (PRAGMA table_info shows column)

5. **Seeder** (`tools/seed_demo_data.py`): Already uses named INSERT with `prompt_sha` column.

**Verification:** 603 tests pass; Alembic up→down→up smoke clean.
tasks.testStrategy:
**Verified via:**
1. `pytest` — 603 tests pass (includes 5 new Task 57 tests)
2. Unit tests in `test_state.py` verify VerdictRecord accepts None (backward compat), accepts 64-char SHA256, and round-trips through JSON
3. Unit test in `test_judge.py` verifies `verdict_records[-1].prompt_sha == load_prompt('judge').sha256`
4. Unit test in `test_migrations.py` verifies PRAGMA table_info shows `prompt_sha` column after upgrade head
5. Alembic smoke: `alembic upgrade head && alembic downgrade 0001 && alembic upgrade head` completes without error

tasks.title: Dispatch regression replay on HALT_REGRESSION_DUE from the graph
tasks.description: When the orchestrator halts with halt_reason='regression_due', the graph should branch into run_regression_session and resume the main loop afterward. Task 40 follow-up.
tasks.details:
**COMPLETED** — Regression dispatch is fully implemented in `src/agentforge_redteam/graph_factory.py`.

**Implementation summary:**

1. **NODE_REGRESSION constant** (line 134): New node name constant exported alongside NODE_ORCHESTRATOR, NODE_RED_TEAM, NODE_JUDGE, NODE_DOCUMENTATION.

2. **_orchestrator_routes three-way router** (lines 290-316): Replaces the old two-way `_should_halt` function with a three-way decision table:
   - `halt_reason='regression_due'` → routes to `regression` node
   - `halt_reason` is any other non-None value → routes to `halt` (END)
   - `halt_reason=None` → routes to `continue` (red_team loop)
   The old `_should_halt` function is kept callable at line 279-287 for backward compatibility with external diagnostic scripts.

3. **_regression_closure async function** (lines 575-610): The regression node implementation that:
   - Reads `new_target_sha` from `run_manifests` table using `session_id` as lookup key
   - Calls `run_regression_session(new_target_sha=..., engine=..., http=..., llm_for_judge=..., langfuse=...)`
   - Escalates P0/P1 'regressed' results to `human_approval_queue` via `_escalate_regressed_to_queue`
   - Clears `halt_reason` via `state.model_copy(update={'halt_reason': None})` to resume the main loop
   - Has defensive guard returning state unchanged if invoked with non-regression halt_reason

4. **_escalate_regressed_to_queue helper** (lines 327-403): Module-level function that:
   - JOINs `regression_runs` to `findings` on `finding_id` to look up severity
   - INSERTs into `human_approval_queue` only for P0/P1 severity findings with 'regressed' status
   - Skips synthetic exception-derived results that have no matching `regression_runs` row
   - Returns count of escalations inserted, logs via structlog

5. **Graph wiring** (lines 638-657): NODE_REGRESSION added to graph with edge back to orchestrator.

**Test coverage (7 new tests in tests/unit/test_graph_factory.py, 22 total in file):**
- `test_regression_due_halt_routes_to_regression_node` (line 753)
- `test_other_halt_reasons_still_route_to_end` (line 764)
- `test_no_halt_routes_to_red_team` (line 781)
- `test_regression_closure_clears_halt_reason_on_success` (line 790)
- `test_regression_closure_returns_unchanged_state_when_halt_reason_differs` (line 838)
- `test_escalate_regressed_to_queue_inserts_p0_p1_only` (line 862)
- `test_escalate_regressed_to_queue_skips_results_without_run_row` (line 974)

Full test suite: 642 tests passed.
tasks.testStrategy:
**Implemented test coverage:**

1. **Router tests** — Unit tests for `_orchestrator_routes` three-way decision table covering all branches: regression_due→regression, other halts→END, None→continue.

2. **Closure behavior tests** — Integration tests verifying:
   - `halt_reason` is cleared after successful regression run (empty corpus path)
   - State returned unchanged when invoked with non-regression halt_reason (defensive guard)

3. **Escalation tests** — Unit tests for `_escalate_regressed_to_queue` verifying:
   - Only P0/P1 severity regressions are escalated to human_approval_queue
   - P2/P3 regressions stay only in regression_runs audit table
   - Synthetic exception results (no matching run row) are skipped safely

4. **Full suite regression** — All 642 tests pass, confirming no regressions introduced.

tasks.title: Run a live red-team campaign against deployed target and promote a finding
tasks.description: Operator action: run start-session against the deployed platform, capture real audit rows + Langfuse traces, promote confirmed findings into evals/regressions/. Produces the live evidence the audit identified as missing.
tasks.details:
**COMPLETED** — fully addressed across 6 evidence packages and a regression replay artifact.

**What shipped:**

1. **Live campaigns against the deployed platform** (https://104-248-232-22.sslip.io/) — 5 packaged sessions:
   - `29488fc5` (initial wedge — surfaced bugs)
   - `e2590f4c` (post-mutation-fix verification, 2 findings landed)
   - `ebd35d75` (post-cost-batch verification)
   - `f044fd18` (cache verification, 9 findings, 95.5% Sonnet cache hit rate)
   - `b1a35acc` (multi-turn + 6-category verification, all 6 categories visible in coverage_matrix)

2. **Promoted regression cases** — 6 cases under `evals/regressions/`, replayable end-to-end via `agentforge-redteam regress`. SHA-keyed rubric cache (`evals/regressions/.rubric_cache/`) ensures replay scores against the historical rubric, not the current one.

3. **Replay artifact** — `docs/EVIDENCE/regression_replay/2026-05-13-replay-output.txt` captures a clean fresh-checkout replay (held=6, regressed=0, no FK errors).

4. **Live droplet replay** — populated `regression_runs` on the droplet via real-Judge replay against the deployed target. `GET /regressions` now returns 6 rows visible in the SPA's REGRESSIONS page.

5. **Per-session evidence files** — each session dir has session_metadata.json (DB SHA + git SHA provenance), cost_summary.json (per-agent breakdown + cache stats), findings.json, coverage_matrix.json, multi_turn_transcripts.json, and a README mapping the run to reviewer concerns.

6. **Honest gap** — stretch-category regression promotions still pending. Both Cat-4/5/6-only campaigns produced 0 findings (deployed Co-Pilot defends those attacks robustly). Documented in `docs/BUG_LEDGER.md` Diagnosis-only section.
tasks.testStrategy: Verified live: (1) `curl https://104-248-232-22.sslip.io/coverage` returns rows from all 6 categories, (2) `curl https://104-248-232-22.sslip.io/regressions` returns 6 rows after droplet replay, (3) fresh-checkout `uv run agentforge-redteam regress --target-sha audit-check --root evals/regressions` reports total=6, held=6, regressed=0, no IntegrityError.

tasks.title: Upgrade deployed UI to HTTPS with a real domain + certbot
tasks.description: Replace the HTTP-only nginx config currently serving the operator UI at http://104.248.232.22/ with the committed TLS config (deploy/nginx.conf), pointed at a real domain with Let's Encrypt.
tasks.details:
The MVP deploy uses HTTP-only on the droplet IP (deploy/nginx-http-only.conf) to ship fast. This task hardens it: (1) Pick a domain. Add an A record pointing to 104.248.232.22 with DNS provider; confirm propagation via 'dig +short <domain>' returning the IP. Cloudflare proxy OFF during certbot run. (2) ssh root@104.248.232.22 and: cp /srv/agentforge-redteam/deploy/nginx.conf /etc/nginx/sites-available/agentforge-redteam (overwrites the http-only config); sed -i 's/platform.example.com/<your-domain>/g' /etc/nginx/sites-available/agentforge-redteam; nginx -t && systemctl reload nginx. (3) certbot --nginx -d <your-domain> --redirect --agree-tos -m <your-email>. certbot rewrites the server block in place to add the SSL listener + the 301 redirect from port 80. (4) Verify: curl -I http://<domain> returns 301 to https; curl -I https://<domain>/healthz returns 200; the BasicAuth still prompts. (5) Optional: enable HSTS preload after a few days of stable cert renewal (already set in deploy/nginx.conf at 1y; add preload tag once confident). (6) Update README.md MVP Status section: replace the IP URL with the domain URL. Acceptance: https://<domain>/ui loads the operator UI with a valid TLS cert; HTTP redirects to HTTPS; certbot.timer is enabled (systemctl list-timers | grep certbot).

**COMPLETED 2026-05-12** — Cert is live at https://104-248-232-22.sslip.io/ (sslip.io wildcard hostname mapping the droplet IP, not a custom domain). /healthz returns 200; HTTP redirects to HTTPS. Shipped via commits f69e586 (feat(deploy): TLS via Let's Encrypt against sslip.io hostname) and d5d6347 (fix(deploy): drop HTTP/2 from TLS listener — middlebox compat).
